title: Architecture Exploration
description: Explore how M1 SDK Contract Preview fits existing architecture. Map crates, module boundaries, dependency graph, DB/run integration, and where plugin-sdk/plugin-host should live.
findings: Architecture maps cleanly to new revdeck-plugin-sdk for public manifest/ObjectBatch contracts and revdeck-plugin-host for dry-run, permission checks, runner skeleton, and analysis-run audit. Reuse ObjectRef/StableObjectKey and AnalysisRunRepository/ObjectRepository; CLI stays thin. Main risks: no explicit toml/semver deps, dry-run vs mutation separation, broad reindex deletion, coarse run state.

title: Implementation Exploration
description: Explore implementation patterns: Cargo workspace conventions, CLI subcommands, serde/config validation, error handling, test fixtures, and 3+ similar features to follow.
findings: Implementation exploration recommends new revdeck-plugin-sdk/host and tests/plugin-sdk; CLI uses nested PluginCommand; manifest/ObjectBatch validation follows structured validation reports; host reuses ObjectRef/StableObjectKey and analysis diagnostic patterns. Main gaps: toml/semver/schema deps, process runner/sandbox, validate-before-write transaction safety.

title: Integration Exploration
description: Explore integration points for revdeck plugin validate|inspect|test, ObjectBatch dry-run, process host skeleton, analysis runs, diagnostics, and report/TUI future surfaces.
findings: Integration points: add nested revdeck plugin CLI in revdeck-cli; SDK types in revdeck-plugin-sdk; host dry-run/process/audit in revdeck-plugin-host; plugin_runs audit schema in revdeck-db. ObjectBatch commit needs transaction/rollback tests; TUI/report should consume committed objects now and avoid latest_analysis_run pollution for plugin state.

title: Risk Exploration
description: Explore risks: plugin safety, permission modeling, backwards compatibility, deterministic replay, Windows process behavior, schema stability, security surface, and test coverage gaps.
findings: Risks: no existing runner/sandbox/redaction; permission model must be default-deny and scoped as skeleton. ObjectBatch needs pure dry-run plus transaction for any commit. analysis_runs cannot model full F-006 lifecycle. Replay must normalize paths, CRLF, timestamps, DB IDs, and output. Add plugin-sdk tests for manifest, permissions, denial, replay, Windows behavior.

title: Plan Generation
description: Consume all exploration findings and the prior brainstorm. Generate plan.json and .task/TASK-*.json for M1 SDK Contract Preview with waves, dependencies, read_first files, convergence criteria, and verification commands.
findings: Plan generated for M1 SDK Contract Preview: 7 tasks across 4 waves covering workspace deps, plugin-sdk manifest/ObjectBatch, DB audit migration, plugin-host dry-run/runner skeleton, CLI plugin commands, conformance fixtures, README docs, and full verification.
